item.dc:creator: Come2Japan

item.title: Body painted myself as Jack Skellington - MCroft07
item.description: Really proud of this, wanted to post it here too :) I did paint Jack about 7 years ago. But wanted to repaint it. I did a stupid thing back then and a company asked for the rights to the video for promises of exposure! (Very dumb, I know, but I didn't know better at the time.) Decided to repaint it and try to improve it as much as possible so I could have it back for myself :) Thanks to the people who are super kind and not inappropriate on my posts, it's appreciated. Faq: I use body paint and do these in a mirror It's from The Nightmare Before Christmas No, it is not AI. I use professional body paint covers I paint these by looking in a mirror It takes between 6-10 hours each Body painting myself is my hobby/passion I do own clothes If you want to see more I am MCroft07 on IG, twitter, Tiktok, YouTube I paint them live on twitch I desaturated the video a little bit at the end so it wasn't so jarring when I had my mouth open for the lip syncs and it was bright pink I like doing the lip syncs, they're fun for me while I paint them The reason why there is a disconnect between the head and the neck is purposeful. I have had to create a "round" head and used the maximum width possible for my own head to make the illusion work. If I paint an extended neck it will look wrong when my head is turned. It's fine anyway, I pretend it's a shadow, and alsohe can remove his own head in the film.
item.dc:creator: MCroft07

item.title: Cons of Mass Deportation
item.description: It seems like if they can’t find workers to milk the cows or operate the milking machines, they won’t be able to afford the costs of feeding and caring for the cows—or the bulls that keep them in calf. As a result, dairy farmers will go out of business, along with arable and fruit farms, among others.
item.dc:creator: sodpounder

item.title: Oops LOL
item.dc:creator: Samoela

item.title: My wife's last time stepping foot in her father's house.
item.description: She's sitting where he sat in his chair for the last three years as he slowly deteriorated from cancer before passing away back in March. We're equally sad and relieved at the same time as cleaning out the house has been a stressful ordeal and glad that it's over with. Sunday was our last time there. It's being sold in a few days.
item.dc:creator: DarthPiette

item.title: IM IN REMISSION!! Fuck yess
item.description: Posting a pic of my new choker because Im so happy!! IM FINALLY CANCER FREE Goddess its been an excrutiating battle, but theres light!!! Whoohooo!!!! Hug your pets or plants or whatever for me- share in the happiness Im sending to you all. Im still in shock, but soooo fucking relieved and elated. Finally finally finally. Now onto the healing and maintenance stages. FUCK CANCER ?????????????????? (originally posted by @WitchyVixen on 2023-11-13 08:36:11)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.dc:creator: blonderengel

item.title: Little Japan rant I stand by….
item.description: As a foreigner who has lived in Japan for a long period, I totally agree with this. Japan definitely has aspects of the wonderfully weird & wacky. There plenty of places to let loose and have fun. But at its core, it is polite, respectful, & practices common courtesy to others in public spaces. Try to imagine it from the opposite prospective…. What if loads of tourists flocked to your city, they don’t speak your language, don’t really know anything about your culture, and come to your holy place of worship unannounced to film some kind of dance or content… You might not be very happy with that situation… I can’t recommend traveling to Japan enough. It is an incredible experience sure to create life long memories. That said, having common sense to realize you are a visitor to someone else’s place of worship is essential. Ok rant done… Have a great day on Imgur & Love from Japan!
item.dc:creator: Come2Japan

item.title: It's gone...
item.description: I'm in shock. I checked a couple of months ago. Still owed like $30,000 and then I get a notice saying I've overpaid. It's just gone. I applied for Loan forgiveness like a year ago. I figured with everything going on it was a long shot. But it's like free lotto ticket, might as well play my odds... Still can't believe it. Here's hoping it stays gone these next four years.
item.dc:creator: Junktrunkjunkie

item.title: Disney Villains (from Concept Art to Final Film versions): 1937 to 2023
item.description:
Sleeping Beauty (1959) - Maleficent
Snow White (1937) - Grimhilde the Evil Queen
Cinderella (1950) - Lady Tremaine, the Wicked Stepmother
Alice in Wonderland (1951) - The Queen of Hearts
Peter Pan (1953) - Captain Hook
101 Dalmatians (1961) - Cruella De Vil
The Jungle Book (1967) - Shere Khan
The Great Mouse Detective (1987) - Ratigan
The Little Mermaid (1989) - Ursula
Beauty and the Beast (1991) - Gaston
Aladdin (1992) - Jafar
The Lion King (1994) - Scar
Pocahontas (1995) - Governor Radcliffe
The Hunchback of Notre Dame (1996) - Judge Claude Frollo
Hercules (1997) - Hades
Mulan (1998) - Shan Yu
Tarzan (1999) - Clayton
The Emperor's New Groove (2000) - Yzma
Atlantis: The Lost Empire (2001) - Commander Lyle Tiberius Rourke
Treasure Planet (2002) - Scroop
The Princess and the Frog (2009) - Doctor Facilier
Tangled (2010) - Mother Gothel
Frozen (2013) - Hans
Wreck-It Ralph (2014) - King Candy
Wish (2023) - Magnifico
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: What a great feeling!
item.description: As the image says! After three and a half months I finished a polished first draft of my very first book. Now on to the next phase! If anyone reading this is into scifi adventure mystery space opera shenanigans and would like to help me beta read after revisions, please hit me up! The target audience is YA (16+?) so if you're a minor, do ask an adult if you can contact me first, please. I'll open a dedicated discord server once revisions are done as well! EDIT: Well, this sure blew up, didn't it?! Thanks for all the kind words and nuggets of advice! Be fantastic to one another. And go read more!
item.dc:creator: anonymousdad2

item.title: Mega Lego Train Set
item.dc:creator: DOcelot1

item.title: Here we go again. I got this.
item.description: Back in January I fell off the wagon and fell harddddd. Life got so chaotic, overwhelming, scary, so much change in such a short time. In June I was miserable where I was and the situation i was in. I then pulled myself from a terrible relationship, to then get hard eith life changing news. I didn't take it well and started drinking heavier. At the same time I started a career after years of job hunting, and going back to school. My life outside of work was still miserable and anxiety inducing. Stress from the realtionship I ended didn't end with the relationship. I started drinking more to the point I wasn't sure if I could keep the impact of it from those I loved, I feared loosing my job. I'd be hungover at work and hide it as best as I could. I gained 30 lbs from drinking and stress. Today I am 1 month and 1 day sober. I feel so much better, clear headed, I can do my job, I started back at the gym. This is my first time getting back on the wagon, but I plan to make this my last. The doc told me today I might have to get my liver and ultrasound to check for scaring. Things in life still aren't where I want them to be, but that's fine. I'm sober, I can take things one step at a time on sturdy legs. I have incredible friends and family and it helps a lot. I don't share with them how far I fell, but they're here to help and I love them for it. I know this post is a ramble, but I'm so happy and proud of myself. I thought it was going to be my death. To those that are struggling, please reach out. It's a hard, hard journey. A journey that will never end. You have to want it for yourself. Take it one step at time. Everyone recovers and heals different. If you slip up, try again. Never stop trying, get back up no matter how hard it is. Never stop trying, just one foot in front of the other, one army crawl after the other if your legs won't hold you just yet.
item.dc:creator: puffpuffmcgruff

item.title: John Scalzi’s on 2024 election
item.description: Many of you will recognize Scalzi’s name, but for those who don’t, he’s the author of many great science fiction novels including Old Man’s War. He also has a net positive online presence, IMHO. This is from Threads. Sauce for those who partake: https://www.threads.net/@jscalzi/post/DCPFpSdP7Mv

item.dc:creator: ssumner13

item.title: I Stole This Dump So You Don't Have To! Tuesday Random MEMES that made me chuckle #182
item.description:
1. The Gobermnt
2. Add this operator as a defender on R6 Siege
3. How nice of them to include my Steam Wishlisted Library on this Special Event
4.
5. Hide the pain...
6. You aint fooling me, lady!
7. Doing in the kitchen be like
8. Kid is amazing
9
10
11
12. Why are girls like that :(
13
14
15. It do be like that. Personally I try to make games because I remember that feeling when you were a kid and how much fun it was to sit there and play with your brothers or you friends from school and just have these unforgettable moments that you would discuss even months later like you went on an adventure. I would love to have one of my games make someone younger now have the same experiences. Obviously it would be nice to make money, but the reality is that my first game didn't. I didn't stop and my second game didn't too, but the reactions I've gotten were so heartwarming that I just don't want to stop because games haven't sold well. Currently I'm working on my third one, and if you can help out with a wishlist, that would be amazing: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames
